name: sql
context: DuckDB SQL dialect for analytical queries, friendly SQL features, macros, joins, aggregation, and deterministic reports
info: DuckDB SQL is close to PostgreSQL-style SQL with analytical extensions and convenience syntax designed for fast exploration: direct file references, `GROUP BY ALL`, `ORDER BY ALL`, `SELECT * EXCLUDE`, `SELECT * REPLACE`, `UNION BY NAME`, `PIVOT`, `UNPIVOT`, list/struct types, powerful aggregate functions, macros, and window functions. Agents use this node when writing queries for [[duckdb/cli]] or [[duckdb/python]], especially when transforming files from [[duckdb/files]], producing reproducible reports, or replacing application-side data wrangling with SQL.
triggers: replace a column in select star
body:
# DuckDB SQL

DuckDB's SQL dialect is built for analytical work. It keeps familiar SQL syntax while adding features that reduce boilerplate when exploring wide files, building reports, and reshaping data.

## Direct Sources

DuckDB can scan files directly from SQL. This is part of why [[duckdb/files]] and [[duckdb/cli]] are so effective for agents.

```sql
SELECT * FROM 'events.parquet' LIMIT 10;
SELECT * FROM read_csv('users.csv', union_by_name = true);
SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('logs.ndjson');
```

## Friendly SQL

```sql
-- Group by every non-aggregate selected column
SELECT status, country, count() AS n
FROM events
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY ALL;

-- Drop noisy columns from a wide file
SELECT * EXCLUDE (raw_payload, debug_blob)
FROM read_parquet('events/*.parquet');

-- Replace a column while keeping the rest
SELECT * REPLACE (lower(email) AS email)
FROM users;

-- Align columns by name instead of position
SELECT * FROM january
UNION BY NAME
SELECT * FROM february;
```

## Analytics Patterns

```sql
-- Daily metrics
SELECT
  date_trunc('day', created_at) AS day,
  count() AS orders,
  sum(amount) AS revenue,
  avg(amount) AS avg_order
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'paid'
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY day;

-- Top-N within each group
SELECT
  region,
  max_by(customer_id, revenue, 5) AS top_customers
FROM customer_revenue
GROUP BY region;

-- Windowed running total
SELECT
  day,
  revenue,
  sum(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_revenue
FROM daily_revenue
ORDER BY day;
```

## Reshaping

```sql
-- Long to wide
PIVOT sales
ON month
USING sum(amount)
GROUP BY product;

-- Wide to long
UNPIVOT monthly_sales
ON jan, feb, mar
INTO NAME month VALUE amount;
```

## Macros

Macros make repeated analysis safer than copy-paste SQL.

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO pct(part, total) AS
  CASE WHEN total = 0 THEN NULL ELSE round(100.0 * part / total, 2) END;

SELECT status, count() AS n, pct(count(), sum(count()) OVER ()) AS pct_total
FROM events
GROUP BY ALL
ORDER BY n DESC;
```

## Determinism

DuckDB is free to reorder unordered results. Add `ORDER BY` for reports, tests, and snapshots. Use `ORDER BY ALL` for quick deterministic sorting by all selected columns, and prefer explicit columns when the result is user-facing.

sections.title: Friendly SQL features
sections.body: `GROUP BY ALL`, `ORDER BY ALL`, `SELECT * EXCLUDE`, `SELECT * REPLACE`, `UNION BY NAME`, reusable aliases, `COLUMNS()`, list comprehensions, trailing commas, and direct file references reduce repetitive SQL. These are most valuable during exploratory file analytics and when schema drift makes positional SQL brittle.

sections.title: Analytical aggregation
sections.body: DuckDB includes standard aggregates plus analytical helpers such as `count()` shorthand, `FILTER`, grouping sets, `ROLLUP`, `CUBE`, and top-N aggregates like `max(arg, n)`, `arg_max(arg, val, n)`, and `max_by(arg, val, n)`. Use these to avoid application-side grouping loops.

sections.title: Window functions
sections.body: Use `OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...)` for ranks, running totals, lags, leads, and percentiles without collapsing rows. Window functions are often the cleanest way to compare each row with group-level context before filtering or exporting.

sections.title: Macros
sections.body: Use `CREATE MACRO` for reusable expressions and table macros. Store macros in a persistent `.duckdb` file or a SQL bootstrap file used by [[duckdb/cli]]. Macros are useful for metric definitions, normalization rules, and repeated parsing logic.

sections.title: Deterministic reports
sections.body: Add `ORDER BY` to every report, test fixture, or agent-facing result. Do not rely on scan order from files or tables. Use `LIMIT` only after ordering unless the task is an arbitrary sample. For stable diffs, output JSON or CSV with a fixed selected column order.
